concepts.finding: Self-attentive sentence embedding: 2D matrix of attention vectors (multiple hops of attention) instead of single attention vector. Each attention "hop" focuses on a different part of the sentence. Penalty term encourages diversity across attention hops. Achieves SOTA on author profiling and sentiment classification.
concepts.implication: Multi-hop attention can capture multiple intents within a single query/keyword. A keyword like "best budget running shoes" may have intents related to product comparison, price sensitivity, and shoetype - each attended to by different hops.
concepts.open_question: How many attention hops are needed to capture the range of keyword intents in a typical search query?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-hop self-attention over keyword embeddings captures multiple intent facets and produces better downstream clustering than single-vector embeddings.
